*Name:* Clonca Church
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Donegal
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* Medieval
*Coords:* 55.267882, -7.173547
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clonca_Church_%26_Cross
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Clonca%20Church/@55.283,-7.254,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/CloncaChurch%26Cross.jpg/500px-CloncaChurch%26Cross.jpg

*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* update google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/6b67oxqSpppKHN71A


*Description:*



Clonca Church & Cross is a historic monument located in Inishowen, County Donegal. It is one of the most important and popular historical sites in Inishowen. At 54°43′05″N it is the northernmost of Ireland's National Monuments.

_Listing 1 of 234_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Lynch's Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* wild atlantic way
*County:* County Galway
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 53.4801618, -6.7325901
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Summerhill_House
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Lynch's%20Castle/@53.272,-9.053,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/Summerhill_House%2C_Main_front.jpg/330px-Summerhill_House%2C_Main_front.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* idk

*Description:*
Summerhill House was a 100-roomed Palladian house in County Meath, Ireland which was the ancestral seat of the Viscounts Langford and the Barons Langford. Built in 1731, it was likely designed by Sir Edward Lovett Pearce and completed by Richard Cassels in the Palladian style, although Sir John Vanbrugh, who was related to Pearce and with whom he trained, is thought to have also influenced the design of the house, which could be seen by the Baroque details, great arched chimney stacks and the palatial grandeur and scale.

_Listing 2 of 234_

listing.name: Lynch's Castle
listing.desc: Summerhill House was a 100-roomed Palladian house in County Meath, Ireland which was the ancestral seat of the Viscounts Langford and the Barons Langford. *Description:*
Carrigaholt is a small fishing village in County Clare, Ireland, a castle and a Catholic parish by the same name. The area was officially classified as part of the West Clare Gaeltacht; an Irish-speaking community; until 1956.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Clonfert Cathedral
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* wild atlantic way
*County:* County Galway
*Type:* cathedral
*Period:* 6th-12th century
*Coords:* 53.240651, -8.058621
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clonfert_Cathedral
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Clonfert%20Cathedral/@53.228,-8.068,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMNV9RrPghV_Tr2qCCC0pevreYBXaCWupD5C7cwIR968MiKjpUbcwuNOpnjCspeaQABQVwNLxIqSmOW6PrGKVBpeht3HG3XTsQPcmdL0Ra_WhdBNHVsUaxS1AZKAXH8kT1s63nfd-No5pBRHw=s4800-w800
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Clonfert Cathedral is a cathedral of the Church of Ireland in Clonfert, County Galway in Ireland. Previously the cathedral of the Diocese of Clonfert and then one of three cathedrals in the United Dioceses of Limerick and Killaloe, it is now one of five cathedrals in the Diocese of Tuam, Limerick and Killaloe.

_Listing 5 of 234_
